---
title: "No longer resolving names?"
date: 2006-09-12
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by BrianK on 2006-09-12
I've got two servers that no longer resolve names.  If I cat resolv.conf, it says this:

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN


but if I man resolvconf, it says that it is typically used by other prorams.  How do I get this thing to resolve names again?  Neither of these computers have monitors, so a command-line solution is ideal.

:confused:

---

### Post by BrianK on 2006-10-17
FYI: You can edit this file just like you would if it didn't have the warning & the machine will resolve properly once again.  Your edits will be overwritten when the machine reboots.

I recently uninstalled "resolver" through synaptic, but have not rebooted to test if the same thing happens.

---
